news.headline: Inflation data shows lower factory costs in Feb, leading to hopes for interest rate cuts in 2025. Tariff impact uncertain.
news.summary: February's Producer Price Index report revealed that all factory cost inflation measures were lower than expected. This news follows a decrease in the consumer price index data from the previous day, raising hopes for interest rate cuts in 2025. Gregory Daco, chief economist for EY-Parthenon, noted that the weak PPI data suggests disinflation was happening prior to tariff implementations. The report also highlighted surging egg prices as a key factor driving pressure on goods prices. As economists analyze this data, there is anticipation about how it may impact the U.S. economy in the future. The recent trends have prompted discussions about potential implications as the trade war continues to escalate.

news.headline: Opdyke Class A, LLC offers preferred equity combining solar+battery for Texas biofuels; reduces emissions.
news.summary: Opdyke Class A, LLC has introduced a preferred equity offering that integrates solar and battery storage to assist a Texas biofuels plant in cutting greenhouse gas emissions. The initiative aims to enhance sustainability and efficiency in the facility. The announcement was made on March 13, 2025, in San Francisco. The collaboration with Plural, an on-chain investing platform, signifies a step towards advancing the global shift towards cleaner energy sources. This move is expected to significantly contribute to reducing environmental impact and promoting renewable energy use in the region.

news.headline: VinFast VF 9 offers worry-free luxury on Canadian roads, focusing on after-sales service and premium driving experience.
news.summary: In Markham, Ontario, the electric vehicle market in Canada is evolving as consumers prioritize reliability, convenience, and premium driving experiences alongside range and performance. The VinFast VF 9 has emerged as a standout choice with its focus on top-tier after-sales service ensuring peace of mind and its offering of a sophisticated, high-tech driving experience. The Canadian electric vehicle market is particularly seeing growth in the segment of large, family-friendly electric SUVs. This shift is indicative of changing consumer preferences, with buyers now emphasizing not just range and performance, but also quality customer service and advanced technology in their electric vehicle choices.

news.headline: REV Group, Inc. (NYSE: REVG) releases 55-page 2024 Sustainability Report focusing on ESG performance for fiscal year 2024.
news.summary: REV Group, Inc. (NYSE: REVG), a prominent manufacturer of specialty vehicles such as fire trucks, ambulances, and RVs based in Brookfield, Wisconsin, has recently released its sixth annual corporate responsibility report. The comprehensive 55-page 2024 Sustainability Report highlights the company's Environmental, Social, and Corporate Governance (ESG) performance for the fiscal year 2024 (November 1, 2023, to October 31, 2024). The report emphasizes REV Group's commitment to sustainability as a core business responsibility beyond being just an initiative.

news.headline: SimForm adds compression molding tool design to its cloud-based simulation software, expanding capabilities significantly.
news.summary: SimForm, a top cloud-based simulation software for mold design, based in Montréal, has recently revealed a notable expansion of its services on March 13, 2025. Initially focused on enhancing the speed of injection mold design, the company has broadened its scope to include support for other types of mold design. This expansion is expected to enhance the company's offerings and cater to a wider range of clients.

news.headline: CODiE Awards, renowned for tech excellence, expand under new ownership in 2025 to spotlight innovation & inclusivity.
news.summary: In Irvine, California on March 13, 2025, the prestigious CODiE Awards, acknowledged for honoring technological excellence, are embarking on a fresh journey with a change in ownership. With close to four decades of history, the CODiE Awards are undergoing a significant expansion this year to showcase a wider array of innovations and talents.

news.headline: US gov creates Strategic Crypto Reserve amidst BTC policy shift, planning to integrate crypto into national finance, sparking debates.
news.summary: The United States government has revealed plans to establish a Strategic Bitcoin and Digital Asset Reserve as part of efforts to incorporate cryptocurrency into the country's financial system. This initiative has sparked discussions surrounding decentralization and optimal investment approaches. The decision has garnered significant attention, with experts and the public deliberating on the implications of this move on the national economy and financial landscape.

news.headline: Forge launches Forge Direct: gamified hub for community growth. Developers can host customizable portals. Enticing over static websites.
news.summary: In San Francisco today, Forge, a prominent platform for community-driven game marketing and engagement, unveiled Forge Direct. This new release allows game developers to introduce personalized, comprehensive community portals that can be hosted on their own domain. These portals can fully replace or supplement a conventional game website. Unlike static landing pages commonly found on game websites, Forge provides developers with an interactive engagement tool that includes quests and various features. This innovation offers developers a user-friendly solution for enhancing player interaction.

The inventor introduced the SILENT CORNHOLE/BAGS to help minimize distractions. This innovation aims to reduce the noise level during games.

Village Farms CEO mentions profitable expansion, reports 11% rise in Q4 sales to $82.55M, despite $10.5M inventory write-down. Adjusted EBITDA loss was $3.5M, excluding write-down EBITDA would have been $7M.
news.summary: During the fourth quarter, Village Farms International, Inc (NASDAQ:VFF) posted impressive sales of $82.55 million, indicating an 11% year-over-year increase, surpassing the anticipated $79.87 million. However, the financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024 were impacted by a $10.5 million non-cash impairment charge associated with non-flower inventory in Canadian Cannabis. This decision was made by management after determining that the inventory did not meet the company's quality standards. The consolidated adjusted EBITDA loss stood at $3.5 million, a notable increase from $700,000 in the previous year. Excluding the inventory write-down in Canadian Cannabis, adjusted EBITDA would have been $7 million. This recent data provides key insights into the company's financial performance and highlights areas for improvement.

news.headline: CUPE's Airline Division opposes Liberal plan to privatize Canadian airports; represents 18,500 flight attendants.
news.summary: In Ottawa, Ontario, CUPE's Airline Division is opposing the Liberal government's plan to privatize Canadian airports. The division represents 18,500 flight attendants nationwide. President Wesley Lesosky stated that airport privatization could endanger union jobs and escalate expenses for travelers, especially amidst challenges posed by President Trump's actions against Canada. CUPE firmly stands against this proposal to protect jobs and services.

news.headline: Baker Hughes to power data centers with NovaLT gas turbines due to increasing AI demand; contract to supply equipment for projects across North America.
news.summary: Baker Hughes Company (NASDAQ:BKR) was awarded a contract by TURBINE-X Energy Inc. to provide its NovaLT gas turbine technology. The NovaLT gas turbine can run on various fuels, including natural gas, hydrogen blends, and pure hydrogen. Under the agreement, Baker Hughes is set to supply equipment like gears and power generation generator technology for multiple data center projects across North America. The deal signifies a significant partnership in the energy sector. Additional details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Palantir partners with Databricks to merge AI platforms for autonomous workflows, enabling secure access to GenAI & ML.
news.summary: Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:PLTR) announced a significant partnership on Thursday with Databricks to merge Palantir’s AI operating system with Databricks’ platform for AI, data warehousing, and data engineering. The collaboration will bring together the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform and Palantir Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) to create real-time, AI-powered autonomous workflows. Through the combination of Unity Catalog, Delta Sharing, and Palantir’s security features, customers will have secure, unified access to GenAI, machine learning, and data warehousing. This integration will provide a scalable and open data architecture, merging Palantir's Ontology System with Databricks’ processing capabilities.

news.headline: NVIDIA & Microsoft team up to introduce neural shading support in DirectX, improving frame rates, image quality, and reducing resource consumption for developers. NVDA shares trading higher.
news.summary: Shares of NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) are seeing an increase in trading on Thursday as the company announced a partnership with Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) to unveil neural shading support in the Microsoft DirectX preview for April. Neural shading, a pioneering development in graphics programming that combines AI with traditional rendering methods, is expected to have a major impact. This innovation aims to boost frame rates, improve image quality, and decrease system resource usage. The collaboration will give developers the opportunity to utilize the AI Tensor capabilities of NVIDIA GeForce RTX GPUs. For further details, the complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Kearney and Purdue University collaborate to boost agribusiness education, aiming to bridge academia-industry gap.
news.summary: On March 13, 2025, Kearney's agriculture and food team announced a collaboration with Purdue University's Center for Food and Agricultural Business in Chicago. The partnership aims to strengthen connections between academia and industry to improve the sector. This initiative will bring together experts from both organizations, leveraging their knowledge and resources. The partnership seeks to address key challenges in the agriculture and food industry and drive innovation. This collaboration demonstrates a commitment to advancing research and development in the field.

news.headline: Edgecore Americas Networking Corporation receives GSA Schedule, making its Open Infrastructure Solutions accessible to Federal Government agencies.
news.summary: Edgecore Americas Networking Corporation, headquartered in Irvine, California, has been awarded the GSA Schedule. This achievement will facilitate Federal Government access to the company's Open Infrastructure Solutions. With this contract in place, federal agencies can now easily procure Edgecore's products and services. This move is expected to enhance the company's reach and impact in the government sector, driving growth and potentially increasing revenue.

news.headline: ClickHouse buys HyperDX to enhance observability solutions. Fastest analytical database company. Acquisition supports speed, scalability.
news.summary: In San Francisco, ClickHouse, known for having the world's quickest analytical database, revealed the acquisition of HyperDX, an open-source observability platform developed on ClickHouse. This strategic move underlines ClickHouse's dedication to providing rapid, efficient, and scalable solutions to developers and businesses globally, including in observability. The acquisition merges ClickHouse's unparalleled performance and scalability with HyperDX's capabilities.

news.headline: Textron Aviation offers high-speed internet for Cessna Citation X and X+ jets, benefiting customers.
news.summary: Textron Aviation introduced a new high-speed internet connectivity option for the Cessna Citation X and X+, based in Wichita, Kansas.

news.headline: ETH's 20% drop may trigger $336M DeFi liquidations; prices to watch $1,857 and $1,780. Calls for RWAs integration to offset volatility.
news.summary: If the price of Ether (ETH) drops a further 20%, it could trigger up to $336 million in DeFi liquidations, with $136 million and $117 million already at risk at price levels of $1,857 and $1,780 respectively. Founder Kevin Rusher of the RWA lending platform RAAC warns that a 20% drop to $1,500 could cause total liquidations of $336 million. The crisis is attributed to a $130 million ETH-backed loan in Sky (formerly Maker). ETH has declined over 15% in the past week, below the 200-day EMA since February, with the RSI at 31, nearing oversold territory. Experts suggest shifting to higher-performing altcoins amid ETH's weak performance. Total ETH liquidations could reach $336 million if prices continue to drop, with significant risk levels identified at $1,857 and $1,780. The current ETH price has fallen by over 15% in the past week, trading below the 200-day EMA and reaching an RSI of 31, close to oversold territory. The potential $1,500 price level poses the highest risk for DeFi liquidations. The article emphasizes the need for a more stable asset integration in DeFi to mitigate volatility.

news.headline: Patronus AI introduces Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge for image evaluation, helping Etsy reduce AI hallucinations.
news.summary: In San Francisco on March 13, 2025, the e-commerce company Etsy is utilizing technology to minimize AI hallucinations in product image descriptions. Patronus AI recently introduced the Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge (MLLM-as-a-Judge), which is considered the first of its kind in the industry. This new technology aims to address and mitigate inaccuracies in AI-generated content.

news.headline: Loto-Québec posts strong results in third quarter of fiscal year 2024-2025, with revenues at $734.4M (+5.1%) and net income at $360.5M (+6.3%). Total revenues from April 1 to December 30, 2024, were $2.238B (+6.0%), with net income at $1.132B (+5.2%). $1.073B in prizes awarded to lottery winners.
news.summary: Loto-Québec had a strong third-quarter performance in the 2024-2025 fiscal year, showing growth compared to the previous year. In the quarter from October 1 to December 30, 2024, total revenues were $734.4 million, up $35.5 million (5.1%), with a net income of $360.5 million, an increase of $21.3 million (6.3%). From April 1 to December 30, 2024, total revenues were $2.238 billion, up $127.0 million (6.0%), and net income reached $1.132 billion, up $55.8 million (5.2%). Lottery winners received $1.073 billion in prizes during the fiscal year. The President and CEO credited the organization's committed teams and efficient management for the positive results.

news.headline: XRP price jumps 7.2% to $2.32 in 24 hours, all-time high at $3.40. Weeklong loss of 11.0% from $2.59 to $2.32.
news.summary: In the last day, the price of XRP (CRYPTO: XRP) surged by 7.2% to reach $2.32, marking a positive turn from the previous week's downtrend that saw an 11.0% decline from $2.59. Currently, the coin's all-time high remains at $3.40. This price movement and volatility can be visually compared through a chart. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Cardano drops 32% after not being included in Bitcoin-only reserve, following 58% surge from Trump's announcement.
news.summary: In the last week, Cardano (CRYPTO: ADA) saw a significant 23% decrease following a strategic decision to exclude it from a crypto reserve. The price of Cardano had surged by 58% after President Trump's announcement on March 2 that it would be included in the reserve. However, in a subsequent move, Trump signed an executive order establishing a Bitcoin-only reserve and placing altcoins like ADA into a non-buying stockpile intended solely for holding and selling purposes. Consequently, Cardano's gains were entirely reversed, dropping from $1.14. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: US imposes sanctions on Iran’s Oil Minister and shadow fleet, escalating tensions further. #sanctions #Iran #US
Surface warship industry sees pivotal growth from fleet replacement and technology advancements, driving demand in leading countries and markets. Report forecasts market trends until 2032.
news.summary: In Dublin, the report "Surface Warships - Market and Technology Forecast to 2032" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The surface warship sector is currently crucial, influenced by the requirements for fleet replacement/modernization, new maritime threats, and technological progress. Key advancements in surface ship technology fall into Operational Design and Material Innovations, and Software-Driven Enhancements categories. This market is projected to grow significantly, with an expected rise in demand for modern warships driven by evolving operational needs and emerging threats.

news.headline: Approximately 75% of VASPs in the EU face compliance challenges with MiCA regulations, leading to potential shutdowns.
news.summary: The Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) requires all virtual asset service providers (VASPs) registered in the EU before 2025 to comply with its requirements this year. However, only around 25% of VASPs are predicted to be able to meet the new regulations, similar to the situation in Estonia where 1,900 companies lost their VASP registrations due to new requirements. MiCA imposes complex processes like AML/KYC, data protection, and cyber resilience, leading to high costs of compliance ranging from 30,000 to 80,000 euros. This poses a significant challenge for small crypto startups and businesses, especially in countries like Poland and the Czech Republic where it was previously easier to obtain VASP registrations. As a result, many small businesses and startups may not be able to comply with MiCA, potentially leading to a significant decrease in VASPs operating in the EU.

PPG wins third consecutive Gallup Exceptional Workplace Award for business strategies aligned with employees.

(NYSE: CCS), a prominent national homebuilder, known for its excellence in online home sales and recognition on America's Most Trustworthy Companies and World's Most Trustworthy Companies lists by Newsweek, shared valuable tips.
